baseline_decision: ARCH-MS-114 Tally No-Go; Tally remains modular code in the monolith

route_inventory.project_binding: body override or required query -> resolve_project
required query authorizes call; poller independently routes each message

writer_inventory.retry: client retry can repeat corpus and triage effects
writer_inventory.transaction: no transaction spans corpus append, triage, and optional inbox insert

writer_inventory.retry: client retry can repeat transcription/extraction, corpus, triage, and queue effects
writer_inventory.transaction: no transaction spans external work and durable effects

writer_inventory.retry: ambiguous retry can reapply task mutations
writer_inventory.transaction: task mutations and inbox triage/status use separate repository transactions

writer_inventory.retry: partial failure/retry can replay a prefix
writer_inventory.transaction: loop of independent task and inbox transactions

writer_inventory.retry: safe only while item identity and project remain stable

writer_inventory.idempotency: check-then-insert by source/external_id without database uniqueness
writer_inventory.retry: concurrent or ambiguous retries can duplicate all upstream effects
writer_inventory.transaction: ingest, apply, notify, dispatch, inbox insert, and status update are not atomic

writer_inventory.retry: source retry can overlap or replay message processing
writer_inventory.transaction: shared poll plus per-message multi-effect pipeline

required_for_reopen: durable operation key and retry state for multi-effect intake
transactional or compensating semantics for confirm/apply

project_scope.route_binding: all eight routes call resolve_project; simulate permits an explicit body override
project_scope.storage_isolation: one SQLite database per Switchboard project
project_scope.poller_rule: the caller project authorizes polling, but each mailbox message is routed through the poller's address index
project_scope.proof: executable probe writes alpha while beta remains empty under concurrent WAL readers

auth_contract.web_edge: existing web authentication and project authorization wrap the monolith router
auth_contract.finding: IngestAuthPort authorizes every standalone read/write against the resolved project; the shared transport auth gate remains mounted.

sqlite_contention.model: one project writer plus three same-project readers and a second isolated project database
sqlite_contention.scope_note: This proves ordinary isolated WAL access, not the unsafe application-level dedupe/transaction semantics.

resource_budget.measurement_scope: current live post-Tally-decision box; Tally remains included in the projectplanner monolith, with Auth, Tasks, Coord, and Deliverables processes active
resource_budget.measurement_source: accepted ARCH-MS-114 live host measurement; no standalone Tally deployment exists after its ratified No-Go
resource_budget.measurement_command: PYTHONPATH=.:src /usr/bin/time -l python3 -c 'from switchboard.services.ingest.app import create_app; create_app(); import intake'
resource_budget.projection_basis: Measured service composition plus intake pipeline imports at 56033280 bytes; 64 MiB systemd MemoryMax leaves 11075584 bytes inside the unit. Upload/transcription remain outside day one and swap is excluded from resident reserve.

G1_route_writer_inventory.reason: All eight routes and seven mutation flows are classified with calls, effects, ownership, idempotency, retry, and transaction semantics.

G2_idempotency_and_retry.reason: Database uniqueness closes source/external races; standalone text intake requires an operation key, serializes first writers, replays success, and durably replays failure without duplicating effects.

G3_project_routing_isolation.reason: Every route resolves project explicitly, storage is per-project, and the executable concurrent probe proves no cross-project writes.

G4_auth_boundary.reason: Standalone IngestAuthPort and shared transport middleware enforce project-scoped read/write authorization.

G5_sqlite_contention.reason: Executable WAL probe requires zero lock errors, isolated project counts, and quick_check=ok.

G6_resource_budget.reason: The thin unit is capped at 64 MiB, leaving 538030080 bytes and preserving the 512 MiB resident reserve.

nogo_exit.next_action: Keep Ingest/inbox in-process. Reopen only after retry/idempotency and standalone auth gates pass and at least 65949696 additional bytes of resident headroom plus safety margin are measured.

go_exit.reason: Independence gates authorize side-by-side build and parity; Caddy cutover remains separately gated.
